families.decision: preserve independently; compare branches, commits, trees, project manifests, database schema, API contracts and report definitions before canonical or feature-recovery decisions
perform branch-aware commit/tree and manifest comparison; similar names, dates and sizes are lineage signals only
compare histories, trees, manifests, configuration and business features; dated and suffix naming cannot establish canonical status
treat as security-sensitive candidates; inspect all refs and history without exposing secrets, then compare identity schema, packages and deployment configuration
candidate frontend/process and backend pairing; map API contracts, data schema and shared history before grouping
compare project manifests, commits and implementation details; framework-version naming suggests lineage but does not prove duplication
preserve as a domain application; inventory nonstandard branch and inspect health-domain data/security before any migration classification
operational umbrella only; classify as sibling integration unless code/history proves lineage; prioritize credential and webhook review
determine upstream/fork provenance, license and local modifications before reuse or family consolidation
